reference_module.purpose: Guidance from GDS GitHub repositories including installation, browser support, accessibility, security and coding standards.

quick_start.rule: Install GOV.UK Frontend using either npm or by copying the precompiled CSS, JavaScript and assets【794161067120215†L24-L34】.
After installation, use examples from the GOV.UK Design System and plan to stay up to date with future releases of the package【794161067120215†L35-L40】.

browser_support.rule: GOV.UK Frontend groups browsers into grades A, B, C and X. JavaScript enhancements are only supported in grade A, B and C browsers【794161067120215†L42-L60】.

Grade A includes the most recent stable versions of major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Samsung Internet and Safari), while grade B covers stable releases in the last six months and grade C covers browsers supporting ES6 modules【794161067120215†L42-L55】.
Assistants should design and test services primarily for grades A, B and C, and provide fallbacks for browsers in grade X【794161067120215†L56-L59】.

accessibility.rule: Using GOV.UK Frontend will help services meet WCAG 2.2 level AA but teams must still check that their services meet accessibility requirements【794161067120215†L71-L80】.
GOV.UK Frontend supports recommended assistive technologies and respects user overrides of colours in browsers【794161067120215†L71-L71】.
When extending or modifying components, ensure that accessibility remains intact and conduct manual testing with assistive tools【794161067120215†L71-L87】.

security.rule: GDS advocates responsible vulnerability disclosure. If you find a vulnerability, follow the security policy to report it【794161067120215†L93-L100】.
Only use supported browsers (grades A–C) when executing JavaScript enhancements to mitigate security risks associated with unsupported browsers【794161067120215†L42-L60】.

contributing.rule: Use a `.govuk-` namespace for CSS classes to avoid conflicts and follow the Block–Element–Modifier (BEM) methodology for naming components【681682623149550†L6-L24】.

Name component folders and files in the singular (for example `accordion`, `backlink`, `button`); only use plural names when a component is always used in groups such as `breadcrumbs` or `checkboxes`【524635023229627†L6-L9】.

Structure component folders with a `README.md`, SCSS files (`_[component].scss` and `_index.scss`), a YAML file describing macro options, a Nunjucks macro, a template file and tests【524635023229627†L12-L39】.

Write CSS following the GDS style guide: use 2‑space soft tabs, avoid nesting selectors, name classes using BEM and store colours as variables instead of hard‑coded hex values【681682623149550†L16-L37】【681682623149550†L168-L204】.

In JavaScript, name files after the component, initialise components using `data-module` attributes, target DOM elements with `govuk-js-*` classes and comment code with JSDoc annotations【57926988052763†L14-L55】【57926988052763†L56-L73】.
Use ES modules with explicit file extensions and prefer named exports over default exports【57926988052763†L123-L143】.
Follow the Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct and civil service code when contributing to GDS repositories【794161067120215†L117-L123】.
Maintain clean commit histories by following the GDS Way guidance on commit messages (summary not cited here) and update the changelog when releasing new versions【637704145815316†L31-L88】.
